key_items: Bitcoin most oversold since 2020 crash with RSI mirroring setups that preceded 50% rebounds to $70K
Bitcoin price plunged below fire sale territory with fear index at 12 (FTX crash levels) suggesting capitulation and potential bottom near $60K mining cost
Bitcoin has dumped all gains since Trump reelection and lost 50% from peak, while macro headwinds persist including potential $900B Treasury liquidity drain and Nasdaq correction risks
reasoning: Bitcoin exhibits extreme oversold conditions and capitulation signals (fear index 12, realized losses below 2022 bear market) that historically precede bounces, yet macro tailwinds have reversed with Treasury cash rebuilds, Nasdaq weakness, and delayed rate cuts creating conflicting signals. The setup mirrors 2020/February 2026 rebounds but macroeconomic friction and institutional profit-taking remain significant headwinds for sustained upside.
risks: Escalating macro deterioration through Treasury liquidity drain and broader equity market correction could trigger another leg down below $60K support, negating the technical oversold bounce opportunity.
